assumptions: optional read-two input is admitted only for paired-end alignment runs
reference index assets stay aligned with the selected aligner backend
default stage assumptions captured
admitted bias-mitigation tools must preserve the governed bias report, summary JSON, and benchmark-facing stage metrics
complexity extrapolation requires sufficient observed unique-read support before estimated_library_size and saturation_estimate can be emitted
the governed complexity_curve artifact is the benchmark-facing tabular curve even when the underlying backend is preseq
default stage assumptions captured
default stage assumptions captured
coverage summaries stay comparable only when target-space selection is fixed
damage tools emit comparable terminal-position evidence from the same aligned BAM input
admitted and planned damage rows preserve governed damage-report, terminal-position-metrics, and parser-output semantics even when backend raw artifacts differ
duplicate-rate estimation requires coordinate-consistent BAM and read-group metadata
admitted BAM duplication-metrics tools must preserve the governed duplicate-count, duplicate-fraction, and estimated-library-size contract
endogenous ratio computed from stable mapping summary counts and host-contig rules
admitted endogenous-content tools must preserve governed total-read, mapped-read, endogenous-read, and endogenous-fraction semantics
all admitted BAM filter tools must preserve the governed retained/removed read accounting contract
active_filters records policy intent even when tool-specific argv differs across samtools, bamtools, and bedtools
reference GC context and aligned read depth are available
admitted GC-bias tools must preserve the governed metrics report, plot, summary JSON, and benchmark-facing dropout metric contract
default stage assumptions captured
default stage assumptions captured
paired-end alignment metadata is available for insert-size inference
admitted BAM insert-size tools must preserve the governed report, histogram, paired-template count, and insert-size summary contract
default stage assumptions captured
length thresholds are deterministic for fixed BAM input
all admitted BAM length-filter tools must preserve the governed threshold, retained-read, removed-read, and filtered-BAM contract
samtools remains the governed baseline for fixture-backed BAM mapping-summary smoke coverage
picard contributes a governed comparison backend for alignment-summary metrics without changing the canonical BAM row metrics
MAPQ thresholds are deterministic for fixed BAM/input index
all admitted BAM MAPQ-filter tools must preserve the governed threshold, retained-read, removed-read, and filtered-BAM contract
admitted BAM markdup tools must preserve the governed marked-BAM, duplicate-metric, duplicate-count, and duplicate-fraction contract
paired read overlap clipping requires consistent template metadata
admitted overlap-correction tools must preserve the governed corrected-BAM, corrected-index, before-and-after audit artifacts, and overlap summary metrics contract
samtools is the governed primary executor for flagstat, idxstats, and stats materialization
multiqc is a governed reporting companion that aggregates governed bam.qc_pre summaries without redefining the canonical row metrics
default stage assumptions captured
default stage assumptions captured
bam_index and reference_fasta are optional inputs that enrich validation identity when present
governed fixtures currently emit refusal codes as errors and no warning-grade findings
contaminant database comparability requires source bundle identity, build backend, and index checksum provenance.
rRNA database comparability requires source collection, build command, index digest, and taxonomy scope provenance.
taxonomy database comparability requires source accession, taxonomy dump, build command, and checksum provenance.
plan certification requires a provenance snapshot that binds stage graph, tools, containers, runtime assets, and input checksums.
layout classification must distinguish independent single-end, paired-end, interleaved, and premerged inputs before safety rules are applied.
OTU identity threshold is declared in the run configuration.
OTU clustering uses reproducible parameters and deterministic centroid ordering.
paired-end libraries must be merged or otherwise reduced to one analysis stream before OTU clustering.
lane concatenation requires stable lane ordering and explicit sample identity preservation.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and corrected_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization across corrected outputs.
error-correction backends may emit tool-native working files, but the governed adapter normalizes corrected FASTQ outputs before downstream handoff.
deinterleaving is layout-only and must preserve pair identity and order.
demultiplexed scientific outputs require fixed barcode kit identity and mismatch policy.
reference index must identify the governed host build used for depletion.
single-end input omits reads_r2, host_depleted_reads_r2, and removed_host_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization.
removed_host_reads_* artifacts carry the reads classified as host by the depletion aligner.
host_depletion_report_json is the canonical governed depletion report; backend-native mapping metrics remain traceable from that report.
contaminant decoy reference is explicitly versioned and separate from taxonomy DB.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and contaminant_screened_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during contaminant depletion.
contaminant_screen_report_json is the canonical governed depletion report; backend-native alignment metrics remain traceable from that report.
rRNA reference bank is versioned separately from host and taxonomy databases.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and rrna_filtered_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input contributes both mates to governed evidence-only adapter inspection.
fastqc inspects the full input set, so sampled-read evidence is not claimed by the canonical contract.
governed benchmark rows must expose adapter_report, detected_adapter_ids, detection_confidence, and detection_threshold fields from the canonical adapter-inspection report.
premerge duplicate detection is not equivalent to aligned duplicate marking and must stay report-only until route evidence is present.
instrument-artifact detection is report-only until a governed route explicitly consumes the artifact class.
prealignment complexity estimates are exploratory unless benchmarked against aligned duplicate metrics.
UMI extraction must preserve read order and pair synchronization.
UMI barcode pattern must match the library design for emitted tags to remain meaningful.
canonical report_json semantics remain comparable across governed UMI extraction runs.
low-complexity filtering must remain deterministic for identical input.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and filtered_fastq_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during filtering.
canonical report_json semantics remain comparable across supported low-complexity backends.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and filtered_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during filtering.
selected index backend must match the downstream mapper or host-depletion backend that consumes the index.
asv inference applies only to amplicon libraries with denoising-aware governance.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end denoising retains mate-aware error modeling through the governed dada2 runtime.
interleaving is layout-only and must preserve pair identity and order.
QC manifest materialization must preserve report-level checksums and tool identities before aggregation.
merge suitability depends on paired-end overlap and library model.
governed report_json is the canonical merge contract and may preserve the backend-native merge log as raw_backend_report_txt for provenance.
input abundance table is produced by an upstream feature-inference or clustering stage.
primer normalization applies only to amplicon libraries with explicit primer governance.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and normalized_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization.
read-name normalization is unsafe for provenance-sensitive workflows unless original headers are recoverable.
adapter-bank comparability requires fixed sequence records, orientation policy, and checksum provenance.
host depletion comparability requires a fixed host build, alias set, and index-producing backend.
primer-bank comparability requires marker regime, orientation policy, mismatch policy, and checksum provenance.
overrepresented sequence profiles are diagnostic and do not mutate reads.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input contributes both mates to sequence ranking.
report_json is the governed read-length report contract and the canonical comparison surface for this stage.
length histogram generation must be deterministic for identical reads.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input contributes both mates to the histogram.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate-level summaries.
qc_json is the governed read-profile report contract and remains the canonical comparison surface for profile_reads.
qc_tsv preserves the seqkit tabular backend summary, while qc_plots_dir contains normalized histogram tables derived from the governed report.
Reference or de-novo chimera detection mode is fixed per run.
Chimera reference database id/version/checksum is explicit when reference mode is used.
paired-end libraries must be merged or otherwise reduced to one analysis stream before chimera removal.
paired-end input may emit r1+r2 outputs; single-end may omit r2 artifact.
report_json is the canonical governed deduplication contract; duplicate_classes_tsv and duplicate_provenance_json preserve class-level and backend-lineage evidence for comparison.
pair repair changes read ordering and must publish orphan accounting before benchmark comparison.
the stage aggregates existing FASTQ QC inputs rather than recomputing primary read statistics.
governed QC artifacts may come from read profiling, adapter inspection, taxonomy screening, validation, or downstream cleanup stages that emit governed QC summaries.
report_json is the canonical governed aggregation contract; multiqc_report and multiqc_data remain backend-native deliverables for human review and downstream inspection.
supported tools must emit read-level taxonomic classifications from the screened FASTQ input.
screen_report_tsv preserves the raw classifier summary while classification_report_json stores the governed taxonomy report contract.
when the selected backend supports unclassified-read export and emit_unclassified is enabled, unclassified_reads_* preserve the reads that remained taxonomically unresolved.
benchmark subsampling requires published seed, sample fraction, and input checksum provenance.
polyG trimming remains a no-op on inputs without terminal G-tail artifacts.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and trimmed_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during terminal trimming.
governed report_json is the canonical trim-polyg contract and preserves backend-native report provenance for downstream comparison.
backend-native reports are emitted as optional raw_backend_report_json or raw_backend_report_txt artifacts when the selected backend supports them.
adapter, polyx, and contaminant trimming policies are explicit in effective params.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and trimmed_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during trimming.
report_json is the governed trim-stage report; backend-native reports are preserved separately when the selected tool can emit them.
unsupported policy and layout combinations must fail during trim planning instead of silently degrading execution.
UDG classification is provided via config/env or inferred deterministically from sample metadata.
execution_policy defaults to policy_derived, which preserves UDG-trimmed ends only for the governed udg_trimmed default trim profile and otherwise applies explicit terminal trimming.
Terminal masking/trimming policy is explicit and reproducible.
Refusal occurs when downstream aligner requires untrimmed reads.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and trimmed_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during terminal trimming.
validation is report-only and may not modify FASTQ payloads
pair_sync_policy defaults to require_header_sync for paired inputs and not_applicable for single-end inputs during effective-parameter resolution
validated_reads_manifest records governed lineage for downstream graph provenance without duplicating FASTQ payloads
benchmark comparisons require identical input hashes, strict-mode policy, and governed validation artifacts regardless of backend-specific native outputs
asset verification must fail closed for world-class-closed plans when any declared asset lock is missing or mismatched.

diploid_calling
Diploid genotype model is valid for the cohort.
Likelihood-based calling is used for low-coverage contexts.
GL caller defaults are locked (calling model, mapQ/baseQ, and depth filters).
GL calling emits provenance with parameter values and source references.
Pseudohaploid random-allele or consensus model is fixed and documented.
Pseudohaploid sampling policy is deterministic via explicit seeded configuration.
Sampling seed and policy are logged in output artifacts.
C>T/G>A and PMD thresholds are explicit and versioned.
Damage filtering must support PMD-based filtering and C>T/G>A masking strategies.
Bias audit section must summarize before/after damage filtering metrics.
